m365-347.attackPerspective: When "Ensure Administrative accounts are cloud-only." is not enforced, an attacker who gains initial access can exploit this identity gap to achieve complete environment compromise, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor access. Identity misconfigurations are the #1 initial access vector in cloud breaches. Modern attack frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK T1078, T1098) specifically target identity weaknesses as the primary pivot point for cloud compromise. This is frequently seen in real-world incidents involving credential stuffing, token theft, and OAuth app abuse.
m365-347.implementationNotes:
1. Sign in to Microsoft Entra Admin Center (entra.microsoft.com) with Global Administrator or appropriate admin role
2. Navigate to the configuration area for "Ensure Administrative accounts are cloud-only."
3. Review the current setting against the recommended state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Allow up to 24 hours for policy propagation across the tenant
6. Validate using PowerShell (Microsoft.Graph), Azure CLI (az ad), MS Graph API or the admin portal
m365-347.validationNotes:
# Validate via Microsoft Secure Score
# Navigate to security.microsoft.com → Secure Score
# Or use: Microsoft 365 DSC (Desired State Configuration) for automated baseline comparison
m365-347.tuningNotes: This is a critical control — implement as-is without relaxation. Any exceptions must be formally risk-accepted by CISO or equivalent.
m365-347.advancedDetection:
Monitor via Entra ID Sign-in logs, Audit logs, Microsoft Sentinel, Identity Protection. Key signals: changes to this configuration appear in Entra ID Audit Logs under 'Directory' → 'Policy'. Create Sentinel analytics rule to alert on modifications. KQL:
AuditLogs
| where OperationName has_any ("policy", "Update", "Set")
| where Result == "success"
| project TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy, OperationName, TargetResources

Modern attack frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK T1078, T1098) specifically target identity weaknesses as the primary pivot point for cloud compromise. This is frequently seen in real-world incidents involving credential stuffing, token theft, and OAuth app abuse.
m365-380.implementationNotes:
1. Sign in to Microsoft Entra Admin Center (entra.microsoft.com) with Global Administrator or appropriate admin role
2. Navigate to the configuration area for "Ensure guest user invitations are limited to the Guest Inviter role."
3. Review the current setting against the recommended state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Allow up to 24 hours for policy propagation across the tenant
6. Validate using PowerShell (Microsoft.Graph), Azure CLI (az ad), MS Graph API or the admin portal
m365-380.validationNotes:
# Validate via Microsoft Secure Score
# Navigate to security.microsoft.com → Secure Score
# Or use: Microsoft 365 DSC (Desired State Configuration) for automated baseline comparison
m365-380.tuningNotes: Consider implementing allow-lists for trusted partner domains rather than blanket blocking. Review quarterly as business relationships change.
m365-380.advancedDetection:
Monitor via Entra ID Sign-in logs, Audit logs, Microsoft Sentinel, Identity Protection. Key signals: changes to this configuration appear in Entra ID Audit Logs under 'Directory' → 'Policy'. Create Sentinel analytics rule to alert on modifications. KQL:
AuditLogs
| where OperationName has_any ("policy", "Update", "Set")
| where Result == "success"
| project TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy, OperationName, TargetResources
